# task-review-cleanup

## What I'm Seeing

`tasks/review/` contains 4 files that are already verified:

- `failed-task-rebuild-convention.md` — verified at ~12:34, still in review/ (timestamp 12:25)
- `failure-learning-convention.md` — verified at ~12:04, still in review/ (timestamp 11:52)
- `seed-skill-gaps.md` — verified at ~12:55, still in review/ (timestamp 12:50)
- `spec-pipeline-cleanup.md` — verified at ~12:03, still in review/ (timestamp 11:52)

The Verifier writes a report to `tasks/verified/` (or `tasks/failed/`) but does not remove the source file from `tasks/review/`. The spec-pipeline-cleanup (`specs/archive/spec-pipeline-cleanup.md`) solves the identical problem for the spec pipeline but explicitly scopes out `tasks/`: "Cleanup of non-spec pipeline directories (e.g., `tasks/`) ... is a separate concern with different lifecycle rules."

This violates the factory's coordination principle: the filesystem IS the state. Stale files in `tasks/review/` signal "awaiting verification" to any agent or human inspecting the pipeline. This will recur on every future verification cycle, accumulating ambiguous state.

## What I Want to See

A cleanup mechanism for the task pipeline analogous to `spec-pipeline-cleanup`. After verification, `tasks/review/{name}.md` should not persist when `tasks/verified/{name}.md` or `tasks/failed/{name}.md` exists. This could be an extension to the existing cleanup function (which already runs post-agent-execution) or a standalone `factory cleanup-tasks` command. The task pipeline lifecycle (review → verified | failed) is simpler than the spec pipeline (inbox → drafting → ready → archive), so the implementation should be straightforward.
